data.title: Web3 Projects Urged to Improve Transparency on RootData Platform
data.description:
Web3 asset data platform RootData has issued a transparency alert on Twitter (X), highlighting missing information from several projects, including Fluid, SideShift.ai, Blackhole, Pharaoh, and Koi Finance. According to ChainCatcher, the alert calls for project teams to submit or update their data on RootData to enhance their transparency scores. RootData also announced its ongoing efforts to monitor and expose projects lacking essential information.

RootData's transparency score is designed to assess the completeness and timeliness of project information, urging project teams to claim their projects immediately. The score ranges from A to F, with lower scores indicating less comprehensive information disclosure and higher risks of misconduct, necessitating increased vigilance from investors.

data.title: U.S. President Donald Trump's Approval Ratings Decline Across Multiple Polls
data.description:
According to Jin10, recent polls indicate a significant decline in U.S. President Donald Trump's approval ratings. An AP-NORC poll shows that 60% of respondents disapprove of Trump's job performance, with only 38% expressing satisfaction. Similarly, a CBS/YouGov poll reports a 60% disapproval rate, while 40% approve of his handling of presidential duties.

The SSRS poll reveals a 64% disapproval rate, marking a new low with only 35% approval. The American Research Group (ARG) also notes a record low in Trump's support, with a 63% disapproval rate and 34% approval. Strength In Numbers/Verasight poll results remain unchanged since February, with 60% disapproval and 37% approval.

Quinnipiac University's poll indicates that 56% of voters disapprove of Trump's job performance, while 38% approve, consistent with early March results. A Fox News poll highlights a 59% disapproval rate, the highest since the start of his second term, surpassing records from his first term.

The Harvard CAPS/Harris Poll shows that 53% of Americans believe Joe Biden performs better on economic issues, compared to 47% for Trump. A Reuters/Ipsos poll attributes a 62% disapproval rate to rising fuel prices and widespread opposition to Trump's war on Iran, with only 36% approving of his performance.

Finally, the UMass Amherst/YouGov poll indicates that 62% of Americans oppose Trump's job performance to some degree, with 71% criticizing his handling of inflation and 61% dissatisfied with his approach to employment issues.

data.title: Metaplanet added 5,075 BTC in the first quarter, bringing its total holdings to 40,177 BTC.
data.description: Metaplanet CEO Simon Gerovich stated that the company purchased 5,075 BTC in the first quarter of 2026 at an average price of approximately $79,898, for a total investment of approximately $405.48 million. The year-to-date Bitcoin return is 2.8%. As of March 31, the company held a total of 40,177 BTC, with a total cost of approximately $4.18 billion and an average cost of approximately $104,106.

data.title: Vietnam Plans Stock Market Support Amid Iran Tensions
data.description: Vietnam's government is preparing to implement measures to support its stock market following a significant decline attributed to escalating tensions in Iran. According to Jin10, the geopolitical situation has led to increased volatility in global markets, prompting Vietnamese authorities to consider interventions to stabilize the domestic financial environment. The proposed measures aim to mitigate the impact of external factors on Vietnam's stock market and ensure investor confidence remains intact.

data.title: Metaplanet Acquires Additional Bitcoin Holdings
data.description: Metaplanet has expanded its Bitcoin holdings with a recent purchase, acquiring 5,075 BTC at an average price of approximately $79,898. According to ChainCatcher, the total investment for this acquisition amounts to around $405.48 million. Since the beginning of the year, Bitcoin has yielded a return of 2.8% for the company. As of March 31, Metaplanet's total Bitcoin holdings have reached 40,177 BTC, with a cumulative cost of approximately $4.18 billion, averaging $104,106 per Bitcoin.

data.title: Japan's Finance Minister Could Influence Yen's Future, Economist Says
data.description: Economist Hideo Kumano has highlighted the potential impact of Japan's Finance Minister Satuki Katayama on the country's monetary policy. According to Jin10, Kumano noted that the relationship between Katayama and the Bank of Japan will be crucial in determining whether the upcoming interest rate hike can successfully strengthen the yen. He warned that if Katayama's government criticizes the Bank of Japan's rate hike, it could weaken efforts to reverse the yen's depreciation. Despite Katayama's inclination towards a more relaxed monetary policy, addressing the rising cost of living remains a top priority for her administration. This task is becoming increasingly complex amid soaring energy prices. Currently, the overnight index swap market indicates a 70% probability of a rate hike by the Bank of Japan in April.

data.title: CBOE Volatility Index Sees Intraday Increase
data.description: The CBOE Volatility Index experienced an intraday rise of 2.1 points, reaching a recent level of 26.67. According to ChainCatcher, this development was reported by Jinshi News.

data.title: Pakistan: No US delegation has arrived yet for talks on Iran.
data.description: A spokesperson for the Pakistani Foreign Ministry stated that no US delegation has yet been confirmed to have arrived for talks (regarding the Iran issue). (Jinshi)

data.title: Pakistan Awaits Confirmation of U.S. Delegation for Iran Talks
data.description: A spokesperson for Pakistan's Foreign Ministry announced that there is no confirmation yet of any U.S. delegation arriving for discussions on the Iran issue. According to Odaily, the statement highlights the current uncertainty surrounding the potential talks.

data.title: Binance Expands AI Agent Skills with 13 New Capabilities
data.description:
According to the announcement from Binance, the platform has launched 13 new AI Agent Skills, marking the largest expansion of the Binance Skills Hub to date. This development allows AI Agents to access the full range of Binance products, including advanced derivatives trading, algorithmic execution, fiat, payments, yield generation, and real-world assets (RWA). Through a unified interface, AI Agents can now execute complete end-to-end workflows, from research and discovery to trading, settlement, and portfolio management, without the need to switch tools.

### New AI Agent Skills

The newly introduced skills cover a wide array of functionalities. In derivatives and algorithmic trading, AI Agents can now access coin-margined perpetual and delivery contracts, interact with Binance’s European-style options market, and enable unified margin management across Spot, Futures, and Options accounts. Additionally, advanced portfolio margin frameworks are available for institutional users, offering expanded asset coverage and higher leverage tiers. The algo trading skill allows for the execution of institutional-grade algorithmic strategies, optimizing price efficiency and minimizing market impact.

### Payments and Account Operations

In the realm of payments and account operations, AI Agents can access Binance's P2P marketplace data, perform instant crypto-to-crypto conversions, and manage fiat on/off-ramps. The Onchain Pay feature enables seamless crypto transactions to external wallets, while the Sub-Account skill facilitates the creation and management of sub-accounts, ideal for institutions and multi-strategy traders.

### Earn, Loan, and RWA

For earning and lending, the Simple Earn skill provides access to yield products across over 300 assets, while the VIP Loan skill utilizes Binance’s institutional crypto-backed lending infrastructure. The Tokenized Securities skill allows access to tokenized securities on the Securities Web3 platform, bridging traditional finance and decentralized finance data.

These enhancements aim to provide a comprehensive and integrated experience for users, allowing for seamless management of digital assets across the Binance ecosystem.

data.title: BitMart Market Daily Report
data.description: According to BitMart's market observations on April 2nd, major assets generally traded weakly today. BTC fell back to the $66,000 level, ETH retreated to around $2,050, and BNB also weakened to around $590. BTC is currently trading at approximately $66,570, with an intraday high of approximately $69,170 and a low of approximately $66,238; ETH is trading at approximately $2,047.82, with an intraday high of approximately $2,163.06 and a low of approximately $2,040.71; and BNB is trading at approximately $588.81, with an intraday high of approximately $620.96 and a low of approximately $587.53. Overall, major cryptocurrencies were generally under pressure today, and short-term market sentiment remains cautious. From a technical perspective, BTC retreated from its intraday high and fell back below $67,000, indicating continued selling pressure. ETH saw a more significant pullback, remaining in a phase of renewed pressure after a period of weak recovery. BNB fell rapidly from above $620, showing that even major altcoins failed to establish independent strength. The current market appears to be undergoing a rebalancing process after a rebound, with funds generally maintaining a defensive stance. BitMart X Insight: The renewed defensive sentiment in the market is noteworthy today. BTC, ETH, and BNB all retreated significantly from their intraday highs, indicating that the market doesn't lack rebound action, but rather funds to sustain the rebound into a trend. In this environment, funds are more inclined to take profits at higher levels rather than continuously chase prices. If BTC can subsequently regain above $67,000, market sentiment is expected to gradually recover; otherwise, it will likely maintain a weak and volatile trend in the short term. Investors are advised to continue prioritizing risk control, maintain flexible positions, and wait for clearer directional signals. This article is for reference only and does not constitute any investment advice. The cryptocurrency market is highly volatile and risky. Please make rational decisions and manage your personal risk accordingly.

data.title: Nike Shares Drop 15.5% Following Disappointing Fiscal Q3 Results
data.description: Nike shares experienced a significant decline of 15.5% on April 1, following the release of its fiscal Q3 results, which revealed weakening profitability despite surpassing headline earnings expectations. According to NS3.AI, the company's Chief Financial Officer, Matt Friend, projected that Q4 sales would decrease by 2% to 4%, contrasting with analyst forecasts of nearly 2% growth.

data.title: Crypto Trader Closes ETH Positions to Avoid Liquidation
data.description: A crypto trader known as 'Maji Dage' has closed 2,700 ETH positions in the past four hours to prevent liquidation. According to Foresight News, the trader still holds a long position of 5,000 ETH with a new liquidation price set at $2,031.52.

data.title: Ethereum Network Activity Surges to Near Record Levels
data.description: Ethereum's on-chain network activity has seen a significant increase, with daily active addresses reaching 788,000. According to ChainCatcher, the number of new wallet addresses has also risen by 255,000, bringing overall activity close to historical highs.

data.title: Coinbase CEO: Will personally engage in research on Bitcoin's quantum resistance problem
data.description: Coinbase CEO Brian Armstrong stated that he will personally engage in research on quantum-resistant Bitcoin, emphasizing the industry's need to expedite solutions. Coinbase Chief Security Officer Philip Martin previously pointed out that as quantum computing research progresses, the industry needs to accelerate its response to potential threats. He stated that post-quantum cryptography is feasible but still in its early stages, and hasty implementation could introduce new security risks. Furthermore, consensus needs to be reached on issues such as how to handle wallets that haven't completed their migration. In addition, Coinbase has already undertaken related infrastructure upgrades and research, and is encouraging developers and the community to collaborate on quantum-resistant solutions.

data.title: Bitcoin Faces Potential Downside Risk as BTC Risk Indicator Rises
data.description: Bitcoin Vector has indicated that Bitcoin is currently at a critical juncture, where the potential for downside risk may increase. According to NS3.AI, the platform noted that its BTC Risk indicator has not transitioned into a low-risk phase and is instead on the rise again.

data.title: Swiss CPI Growth Slows in March, Falling Short of Expectations
data.description: Switzerland's Consumer Price Index (CPI) for March increased by 0.2%, according to Jin10, which is below the anticipated 0.5% and the previous month's rate of 0.60%. The slower growth in CPI suggests a potential easing of inflationary pressures in the Swiss economy. Analysts had expected a higher rate, reflecting ongoing concerns about inflation trends in the region. The latest data may influence monetary policy decisions by the Swiss National Bank as it assesses the economic outlook.

data.title: Iran Vows Continued Resistance Amid Ongoing Conflict
data.description: Iranian Foreign Ministry spokesperson Baghaei stated that Iran will persist in its resistance as long as the conflict and assaults on Iranian citizens and cities persist. According to NS3.AI, Baghaei emphasized that the nation stands united against what he described as an unjust and aggressive war.

data.title: Web3 Carnival Guide for April Released
data.description: Foresight News posted on X (formerly Twitter) that as the second quarter begins, the highly anticipated Web3 Carnival Summit is approaching. Attendees are encouraged to save the guide to surrounding activities, which will be continuously updated in the coming weeks to help participants navigate the event efficiently and effortlessly.

data.title: Dollar Index Rises Amid U.S. Military Movements in Middle East
data.description: On April 2, Jin10 reported that the dollar index increased during the Asian afternoon trading session as market expectations grew regarding potential U.S. military actions against Iran. According to Jin10, analysts from the Commonwealth Bank of Australia's global economics and market research division noted in a report that the U.S. is reportedly doubling its fleet of A-10 attack aircraft in the Middle East. They added that the movement of military assets and troops to the region raises the possibility of a ground invasion. Given the dollar's status as a safe-haven currency, they remain optimistic about its performance.
